pattern: With lengths capped so small, there's no need for anything clever -- just total up every subarray whose length falls in range and keep the smallest positive total you see.
hints: The allowed subarray lengths are bounded by the array size itself, so you can afford to check every subarray whose length falls between l and r.
For each starting index, extend the subarray one element at a time and keep a running sum -- that avoids recomputing the sum from scratch for every length.
Track the smallest sum you've seen so far that is strictly greater than zero, and only report -1 if no subarray in the allowed length range ever produced a positive sum.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, no positive subarray exists
single element array, l equals r equals 1
l equals r equals full array length
positive sum only achievable at the length boundary

stress.description: random small arrays with random length windows
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100))
    nums = [rng.randint(-20, 20) for _ in range(n)]
    l = rng.randint(1, n)
    r = rng.randint(l, n)
    return [nums, l, r]
